messages.content:
Clinical note:
The patient, a 45-year-old male with a history of Hodgkin lymphoma in remission, presents with progressive right-sided weakness following a cerebrovascular event. Neurological examination reveals significant right hemiparesis. MRI shows findings consistent with discitis in the lumbar region. Follow-up for stroke sequelae, ongoing cancer surveillance, and management of discitis are recommended.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with persistent right hip pain and a recent MRI confirmed findings of osteonecrosis of the right femur. On examination, nipple retraction was noted in the left breast, prompting further investigation. Additionally, the patient was seen for a routine screening mammogram, with no immediate abnormalities detected but further diagnostic work-up planned.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 56-year-old female presenting with recurrent generalized tonic-clonic seizures and cognitive decline, suggestive of Lennox-Gastaut syndrome, currently in status epilepticus. She also reports dry eyes, mouth, and muscle weakness, consistent with Sjogren syndrome with myopathy. Radiographs reveal Paget's disease of bone in the right upper arm, complicating her clinical picture.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with chronic left shoulder pain and swelling, diagnosed as other myositis, accompanied by symptoms of coughing and shortness of breath following exposure to industrial fumes, indicative of chemical bronchitis and pneumonitis. X-rays further revealed an osteophyte in the left hip joint. No signs of acute trauma or infection detected.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of limb-girdle muscular dystrophy due to fukutin-related protein dysfunction, exhibiting progressive weakness in the left forearm. Radiographs reveal a stress fracture in the right hand, sustained during an initial injury. Additional scans indicate decreased bone density in the left forearm.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 68-year-old female with a history of breast cancer and a complete dental prosthetic device presenting for follow-up. She reports a pathological fracture in her right foot, a sequela of her neoplastic disease. On examination, an unspecified lump in the right breast, overlapping quadrants, is palpated. Radiographs confirm the foot fracture and breast examination findings.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The 58-year-old male presents with mid back pain and stiffness, particularly in the cervicothoracic region. He also reports episodes of headache and jaw claudication, suggestive of giant cell arteritis. His behavior is problematic only at home, leading to conflicts with family members, consistent with a conduct disorder confined to the family context.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The 55-year-old male presents with hematuria and proteinuria, consistent with acute nephritic syndrome and diffuse mesangial proliferative glomerulonephritis. He also reports sudden onset of right-sided weakness, which is indicative of a pure motor lacunar syndrome. Additionally, the patient has a history of post-traumatic urethral stricture, likely secondary to a previous pelvic injury.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with progressive shortness of breath and a dry cough, along with a history of follicular lymphoma now showing lymphadenopathy. Chest imaging confirms lymphoid interstitial pneumonia. MRI reveals vertebral artery compression, causing transient neurological symptoms. No specific site for the vertebral artery compression was identified.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The 68-year-old female presents with progressive right hip pain and swelling, consistent with Felty's syndrome. She also reports a history of chronic pelvic pain and has been diagnosed with chronic oophoritis. Family history is significant for osteoporosis, with her mother having been affected.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a painful, swollen left humerus and has a noticeable curvature of the spine. X-rays confirm an aneurysmal bone cyst in the right thigh and acute osteomyelitis in the left humerus. Curvature of the spine is observed, suggestive of a non-specified form of scoliosis.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with a persistent right hip pain, likely due to a bony spur on the iliac crest. Additionally, a suspicious lesion on the left lower eyelid, consistent with a skin malignancy, has been noted and scheduled for biopsy. The patient also reports retained plastic fragments from a prior surgical procedure, currently asymptomatic but monitored for complications.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 68-year-old male with a history of alcohol dependence presenting with visual hallucinations and severe anxiety. He reports worsening bone pain in the right foot, likely due to Paget's disease. Physical examination reveals signs of osteitis deformans with increased foot size and deformity. The patient’s symptoms are consistent with sedative-induced psychosis and unspecified anxiety disorder.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with persistent dysarthria, following a nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage three months ago. Examination also reveals a bursal cyst in the right ankle, causing localized swelling and discomfort. Additionally, the patient reports ongoing gastrointestinal symptoms and is undergoing screening for potential digestive system disorders.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 28-year-old G1P0 at 15 weeks gestation, presenting for routine prenatal supervision. She reports a sudden onset of left-sided weakness and facial droop. MRI confirms a cerebral infarction due to an embolism of the unspecified carotid artery. Pregnancy remains uncomplicated otherwise.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 45-year-old male with a history of chronic alcohol use, presents with joint pain in multiple sites and reports symptoms consistent with sedative withdrawal, including tremors and anxiety. He is currently not taking any sedative medications. Additionally, the patient is here for routine screening for infectious diseases as part of a comprehensive wellness check.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a service member recently deployed to a high-stress combat zone presenting with auditory hallucinations and left shoulder pain. The shoulder pain is exacerbated by arm movements and is consistent with other articular cartilage disorders. Urine toxicology screen positive for hallucinogen use.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with persistent proteinuria confirmed by urine analysis and renal biopsy indicating primary membranous nephropathy. The patient also reports symptoms consistent with opioid withdrawal, including anxiety, sweating, and cravings. Additionally, the patient is here for follow-up and adjustment of a previously implanted cardiac device.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with painful, erythematous nodules on the neck and sacral regions, suggestive of panniculitis. Additionally, she reports right-sided jaw pain and difficulty in chewing, consistent with right temporomandibular joint disorder. Her family history reveals multiple relatives with autoimmune disorders and hematological diseases.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of a transplanted heart and reports episodes of chest pain suggestive of angina pectoris. Coronary angiography revealed atherosclerosis of the bypass graft. Additionally, the patient has a history of rheumatic fever without current cardiac involvement and exhibits moderate vascular dementia accompanied by anxiety. Neurological examination confirms cognitive decline and anxiety symptoms.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with persistent pain and swelling in the left ankle and foot, worsening with weight-bearing activities. X-rays confirmed secondary osteoarthritis in these regions. The patient has a history of acute lymphoblastic leukemia now in remission. Additionally, the patient has a non-healing ulcer on the calf due to varicose veins, which have been previously noted.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient reports chronic sore throat and recurrent tonsillitis. Examination reveals significant tonsillar hypertrophy, necessitating a follow-up for potential tonsillectomy. Additionally, the patient has noted progressive stiffness and pain in the thoracolumbar region, with imaging confirming calcific deposits in the muscles of the left hand.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with complaints of numbness and tingling in the feet and lower legs, consistent with autonomic neuropathy. Additionally, the patient reports pain and swelling along the medial aspect of the left knee, which is diagnosed as tibial collateral bursitis. X-ray reveals a cystic lesion in the left lower leg, confirmed as a bone cyst.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with severe abdominal pain and a history of Crohn's disease, which led to a diagnosis of intestinal obstruction. X-rays confirmed a pathological fracture of the right radius. Additionally, the patient reported chronic lower back pain and deformity localized to the sacral and sacrococcygeal region, likely related to a deforming dorsopathy.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presented with a non-healing ulcer on the medial aspect of the right lower extremity accompanied by significant swelling and discoloration. The patient reported a history of deep vein thrombosis following a prolonged hospital stay for COVID-19. Social history reveals the patient is experiencing unsheltered homelessness, which complicates adherence to treatment and follow-up care.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 65-year-old male with chronic kidney disease, presents with acute onset of severe pain, swelling, and redness in the right knee, consistent with a gout flare. Additionally, the patient reports lower abdominal discomfort and visible varicose veins in the pelvic region, suggesting pelvic varices. Examination confirms joint effusion and tenderness in the right knee, along with palpable varicosities in the lower abdomen.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with acute pain and swelling in the left upper arm, where an ultrasound revealed a spontaneous rupture of the biceps tendon. Additionally, the patient reports a history of chronic gout that has led to the development of tophi, primarily affecting the thoracic vertebrae, corroborated by X-ray findings. Physical examination also noted enlarged lymph nodes in the axillary and left upper limb regions, with recent biopsy confirming other Hodgkin lymphoma.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with persistent left ankle pain consistent with a stress fracture, now in routine healing per follow-up imaging. History of renal impairment complicates gout management, with current symptoms localized in the ankle region. Additionally, the patient is undergoing screening for possible early signs of a nervous system disorder as a preventive measure.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with persistent pain and deformity of the right lower leg. X-rays confirm a pathological fracture of the right fibula with malunion due to metastatic bone disease. The patient is currently awaiting a kidney transplant and has an artificial skin graft on the lower leg.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with severe chest pain, fever, and difficulty swallowing, consistent with mediastinitis. She also reported a sudden, severe headache, and imaging confirmed a nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage. Additionally, she displayed significant calcification and ossification in the muscles of her right ankle and foot, a sequela of extensive thermal burns sustained six months prior.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient reports chronic mid-back pain and stiffness following her MMR vaccination two years ago, consistent with postimmunization arthropathy. X-rays confirm a non-union fracture in the thoracolumbar vertebra, likely a sequela of a previous injury. Additionally, the patient has been experiencing progressive myoclonic jerks and episodes of confusion, without status epilepticus, which have not responded to treatment, diagnosed as Lafora progressive myoclonus epilepsy.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with significant instability and pain in the left knee, consistent with a flail joint. Radiographs reveal marked deformities and bone thickening in the right shoulder, indicating hypertrophy. Additionally, the patient reports progressive pain and deformities in the left ankle, suggesting Paget's disease.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
A 55-year-old female presents with acute chest pain and shortness of breath consistent with Takotsubo syndrome. She also reports persistent pain in her left forearm over the past month. Physical examination revealed enlarged axillary lymph nodes. A biopsy confirmed nodular lymphocyte predominant Hodgkin lymphoma affecting the axillary and upper limb lymph nodes.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with persistent pruritic lesions consistent with diabetic dermatitis on the lower extremities, along with a history of drug-induced diabetes mellitus. She also reports chronic pain and swelling in the right ankle and foot, where a tophus has been palpated, indicative of drug-induced chronic gout. Additionally, she exhibits severe obesity with a BMI of 42, primarily due to excess caloric intake.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 72-year-old male with a history of moderate dementia, presents with chronic symptoms of ethmoidal sinusitis including persistent nasal congestion and facial pain. He also avoids social interactions and appears anxious in clinical settings. His cognitive decline has been stable without notable behavioral or psychotic disturbances.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe urinary retention and dysuria, secondary to a postinfective membranous urethral stricture. A recent cystoscopy revealed a malignant neoplasm on the lateral wall of the bladder. Additionally, there are signs of infection and obstruction at the site of the continent urinary stoma, consistent with complications of the stoma.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with severe abdominal pain, fever, and a palpable mass in the lower abdomen, indicating acute diverticulitis with perforation and abscess. Endoscopic evaluation revealed a malignant neoplasm in the postcricoid region. Laboratory results showed hypocalcemia and hyperphosphatemia, consistent with pseudohypoparathyroidism.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with pain and swelling in the right great toe after recent increased physical activity. X-rays confirm a stress fracture in the right toe. Additionally, the patient reports spontaneous tearing of the flexor tendons in the right ankle, which occurred without trauma. The patient also has a palpable calcified mass in the left thigh, confirmed by ultrasound.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a swollen and painful left elbow, indicative of infective bursitis. Additionally, she reports a noticeable left-sided facial weakness, consistent with previous cerebrovascular disease. On examination, palpable lymphadenopathy is noted in multiple sites, and a recent biopsy confirmed the presence of mature T/NK-cell lymphoma.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of rheumatoid arthritis now complicated by rheumatoid lung disease and involvement of the right hip. Recent imaging confirmed chronic lobular hepatitis due to acetaminophen toxicity. Additionally, a biopsy revealed a malignant neoplasm of the oropharynx. The patient reports dyspnea, hip pain, and jaundice.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Clinical note: A 68-year-old male presents with progressive tremors and rigidity consistent with Parkinsonism. Additionally, he reports numbness and tingling in the lateral aspect of his right lower limb. On examination, a notable history of a prior knee dislocation was confirmed, and there is evidence of unilateral post-traumatic osteoarthritis in the right knee.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with complaints of dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain. A chest X-ray revealed a persistent right pneumothorax, and CT scans showed intrapelvic lymphadenopathy concerning for lymphoma. Laparoscopy confirmed superficial endometriosis of the bladder. A biopsy of the lymph nodes confirmed lymphoblastic lymphoma.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with an incarcerated inguinal hernia and symptoms of bowel obstruction. There is a history of intractable absence epilepsy currently manifesting as status epilepticus. Radiographs confirm a pathological fracture of the right humerus as a sequela of metastatic disease.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a non-healing ulcer on the medial aspect of the left lower leg. Examination reveals evidence of atherosclerosis in the left leg bypass graft and chronic changes consistent with juvenile idiopathic scoliosis in the thoracic region. Additionally, the patient reports ongoing back pain without trauma, with imaging confirming a healed lumbar vertebral compression fracture.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with progressive headaches, confusion, and gait disturbances following a motor vehicle accident two months ago. MRI reveals post-traumatic hydrocephalus. Additionally, CT angiography shows a nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage originating from the left middle cerebral artery. Physical examination confirms swelling and pain over the left knee, consistent with bursitis.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Clinical note: A 56-year-old male presents with acute dyspnea, pleuritic chest pain, and tachycardia. Patient reports recent occupational exposure to benzene. Physical examination reveals signs of acute cor pulmonale and a CT scan confirms pulmonary embolism. Postural kyphosis is noted on his spine examination.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with severe left hip pain and swelling following a fall, indicating a rupture of the synovium. He also reported experiencing hallucinations and tremors, consistent with alcohol withdrawal with perceptual disturbance. Additionally, he complained of persistent hoarseness and throat irritation, diagnosed as chronic laryngitis.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a persistent locking sensation in the middle finger of the right hand, diagnosed as trigger finger. Additionally, the patient reports chronic pelvic pain and dyspareunia, with ultrasound confirming deep endometriosis of the left ovary. On examination, a simple rectal fistula was identified and requires initial management.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting, indicative of a perforated peptic ulcer. Additionally, there is a history of contact with industrial solvents potentially contributing to this presentation. Physical examination reveals tenderness in the right hand consistent with osteitis condensans, and a history of exposure to unspecified hazardous chemicals is documented.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with delayed puberty, involuntary vocal tics, and motor tics predominantly affecting the face and shoulders, which have been ongoing since age 7. Also noted is a noticeable curvature of the spine in the thoracic region, first observed at age 12. Examination confirms the presence of juvenile idiopathic scoliosis.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe, recurrent left-sided facial pain consistent with trigeminal neuralgia. Additionally, she reports a noticeable left ankle contracture that has been gradually worsening over the past six months. Examination reveals a sacral biomechanical lesion which may be contributing to her chronic pain and mobility issues.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of rheumatic fever and reports recent joint pain in the left hand, particularly in the metacarpophalangeal joints. Echocardiographic findings indicate valvular changes consistent with rheumatic disease of the endocardium. Additionally, the patient was recently diagnosed with diffuse follicle center lymphoma involving the intrapelvic lymph nodes following an imaging study. Swelling and restricted movement of the left hand joints are noted.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with chronic swelling and pain in the left lower extremity, indicative of deep vein thrombosis. Additionally, she reports a persistent rectovaginal fistula, confirmed by recent imaging and physical examination. On examination, a palpable mass in the lower-outer quadrant of the right breast was noted, which was suspicious for malignancy. Biopsy results pending.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with poorly controlled blood glucose levels following the initiation of corticosteroids for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease. She reports blurred vision and occasional confusion, which she attributes to recent changes in her medication. Urinalysis reveals microscopic hematuria, and a renal ultrasound confirms the presence of a kidney stone. Laboratory results indicate persistent hyperglycemia and signs of diabetic retinopathy with macular edema.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient reports persistent pain and swelling in the right upper arm with limited range of motion. X-rays revealed decreased bone density in the humerus. Additionally, the patient has a documented history of a left total knee replacement and presents with symptoms of urethral stricture, confirmed by urethroscopy after a previous urinary tract infection.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with dysuria and burning sensation during urination, along with a history of chronic lower back pain radiating to the left leg. MRI findings indicate lumbar spondylosis and discitis in the sacrococcygeal region. No signs of acute infection or systemic inflammatory response are noted.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 22-year-old female with a history of anorexia nervosa, restricting type, who presents with significant weight loss and persistent food restriction. She reports difficulty concentrating following a cerebral infarction six months ago. On examination, she has a pathological fracture in the right ankle and foot with malunion, likely due to osteoporosis exacerbated by her nutritional deficiencies.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe lower abdominal pain, nausea, and a distended abdomen. An abdominal CT reveals an acute appendicitis with perforation, localized peritonitis, and gangrene, forming an abscess. Additionally, the patient has a history of ulcerative colitis complicated by an abscess formation. MRI findings indicate a connective tissue stenosis of the neural canal in the abdomen.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe pain and swelling in the left lower leg, consistent with clinical findings of infective myositis. He also reports a recent episode of transient right-sided weakness and difficulty speaking, suggestive of a recent cerebral infarction. Additionally, the patient has a recurrent bilateral inguinal hernia with episodes of bowel obstruction but no signs of gangrene.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with left knee pain and swelling, following a recent bacterial infection, now exhibiting signs of arthritis. The patient also reports concerns regarding their sexual behavior and orientation, seeking counseling for these combined issues. Additionally, the patient reports right elbow pain and tenderness, consistent with bursopathy.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a non-healing ulcer on the left lower extremity, concerning for atherosclerosis of the bypass graft. An X-ray shows a stress fracture of the right humerus, currently in routine healing. Additionally, the patient reports chronic gouty symptoms in the right wrist, with visible tophi formation.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of eclampsia during her last pregnancy and currently reports chronic gout with noticeable tophi in the left foot. He also complains of swelling and tenderness in the left testicle, diagnosed as an inflammatory disorder. Physical examination and history support these chronic conditions.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with persistent deficits in frontal lobe function and executive control, following a previous cerebrovascular event. A recent PET scan revealed lymphoblastic lymphoma involving inguinal and lower limb lymph nodes, as well as non-follicular lymphoma in the intrapelvic lymph nodes. Neurological examination supports cognitive impairments consistent with frontal lobe involvement.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with progressive lower limb weakness and sensory disturbances. MRI reveals demyelination consistent with myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody disease. X-ray shows a delayed healing stress fracture of the left tibia, managed conservatively. Additionally, the patient reports chronic pelvic pain with pelvic ultrasound indicating endometriosis of the bilateral pelvic sidewall.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a non-healing ulcer on the left ankle, accompanied by significant inflammation and varicose veins in the left lower extremity. Additionally, she reports a history of a pathological fracture in the left humerus, currently asymptomatic but with sequelae evident. She also complains of severe flu-like symptoms, including fever and myalgia, diagnosed as influenza due to other identified influenza virus with other manifestations.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with severe polyarthritis in multiple joints, likely due to a bacterial infection. On examination, the left hand shows signs of bursopathy with noticeable swelling and tenderness. Additionally, the patient reports left-sided lower back pain radiating down the left leg, consistent with sciatica.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 72-year-old female with a history of osteoporosis who presented with persistent pain and swelling in her left hand after a previous fracture that has failed to heal. She also reports recent visual disturbances and a palpable mass in the right cornea, confirmed by ophthalmic examination. Additionally, the patient experienced episodes of abdominal distension and reduced bowel movements, with imaging showing signs of partial intestinal obstruction.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with significant anxiety, which appears secondary to a known physiological condition involving chronic kidney disease. She also reports a spontaneous rupture of the flexor tendons in her left shoulder, with physical examination confirming this injury. Additionally, she has a history of hypertension secondary to renal disorders, currently managed with antihypertensives.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient reports persistent pain and stiffness in the right index finger following an old injury sustained three months ago, consistent with a stress fracture sequela. Additionally, the patient presents with tenderness and erythema in the sacral region, suggestive of osteomyelitis. The patient also exhibits multiple somatic complaints without clear pathological findings, indicating undifferentiated somatoform disorder.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with significant pain and decreased range of motion in the right forearm, suggesting osteopathy likely secondary to an underlying systemic disease. The left knee exhibits complete ankylosis with no movement. Additionally, the patient reports atrophy of the vulva, which has been confirmed upon physical examination.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 35-year-old female, presented with symptoms of balanitis, including redness and swelling of the prepuce. Additionally, she reported persistent pain and deformity of the left ankle, previously fractured due to metastatic bone disease. The patient recently delivered quadruplets via cesarean section. On examination, the ankle showed signs of malunion.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented for follow-up regarding their prosthetic arm fitting following a recent stroke, which resulted in an occlusion of the bilateral anterior cerebral arteries. The patient reports difficulty adjusting to their prosthetic and has a non-union pathological fracture of the right middle phalanx from a previous fall. Examination confirms ongoing need for prosthetic adjustments and stable fracture healing.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 35-year-old female with a history of morning wrist stiffness and swelling, diagnosed as other specified rheumatoid arthritis. She also reports episodes of excessive daytime sleepiness and sudden muscle weakness consistent with narcolepsy with cataplexy. Additionally, she has been experiencing intermittent wheezing and shortness of breath, indicative of unspecified asthma.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The 68-year-old male presented with persistent back pain and was found to have thoracic aortic ectasia upon imaging. X-rays of the right upper limb revealed a pathological fracture of the right ulna with evidence of prior malignancy. Additionally, he had a subsequent encounter for a previously fractured humerus with malunion due to osteoporosis, now experiencing limited range of motion and pain.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 38-year-old female with a history of opioid use, presented with severe, diffuse headache, confusion, and vomiting. Additionally, she reported heavy, irregular menstrual bleeding over the past several weeks. A neurological examination confirmed signs of cerebral edema, while laboratory results showed significant anemia likely due to excessive premenopausal bleeding. Opioid dependency and associated mood disturbances were noted during the interview.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with persistent pain and limited mobility in the right knee, secondary to a prior periprosthetic fracture. The patient also reports recurrent dislocation of the right hallux. Additionally, the patient has an ileostomy due to previous bowel surgery. Examination reveals signs consistent with both conditions, and the patient’s symptoms have been ongoing since the initial surgeries.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presented with a pathological fracture of the left humerus, following a minor fall. This injury occurred in the context of known leukemia currently in remission. Additionally, the patient reports chronic synovitis and tendon pain in the left hand, which has persisted despite treatment. Follow-up imaging confirmed the fracture, and malignancy workup showed no evidence of active disease.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient is a 72-year-old female with a history of lymphoid leukemia now in remission. She presents with a right shoulder pathological fracture due to osteoporosis following a minor fall. She also exhibits persistent aphasia, likely resulting from a previous cerebrovascular event. No acute changes are noted in her neurological examination.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with sharp retrosternal chest pain and a history of recurring neck stiffness and inflammation over the past year. An echocardiogram shows pericardial effusion consistent with acute nonspecific idiopathic pericarditis. The patient also reports a personal history of non-radiographic axial spondyloarthritis, managed previously, with no current imaging evidence of structural damage but persistent cervical pain.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient, a 32-year-old male, presents for preconception counseling as a gestational carrier for his partner. He reports left-sided weakness consistent with past cerebrovascular disease and is currently on medication known to affect sperm count. Semen analysis confirms oligospermia, and he seeks advice on optimizing his fertility for surrogacy.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with dyspnea and chest pain, likely secondary to a malignant pleural effusion. X-ray confirms a pathological fracture of the left ankle, showing no signs of healing since the initial presentation six months ago. MRI results indicate progressive leukoencephalopathy, consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic changes.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with noticeable cervicothoracic kyphosis secondary to a previous spinal fusion surgery. On examination, there is evidence of arthropathy in the hands consistent with a history of intestinal bypass surgery. Additionally, the patient reports the functionality of an implanted neurostimulator for chronic pain management.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
Patient presents with progressive heterotopic ossification in the right forearm consistent with myositis ossificans. Swelling and decreased range of motion at the left elbow suggest synovial involvement. Additionally, the patient reported close contact with a confirmed COVID-19 case two weeks prior.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient reports a history of cannabis use, now in remission, but still experiences occasional hematemesis indicative of an esophageal ulcer. Gastroscopy confirms a bleeding ulcer in the esophagus. Additionally, follow-up imaging and biopsy reveal nodular lymphocyte predominant Hodgkin lymphoma in the intrapelvic lymph nodes.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The 72-year-old patient presents with an abscess of the tendon sheath in the left upper arm, accompanied by localized swelling and tenderness. X-rays confirm a pathological fracture due to age-related osteoporosis at an unspecified site, initially encountered. Additionally, signs of osteomyelitis are observed at a different site with symptoms of fever and malaise.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient reports experiencing severe, unilateral, throbbing headaches without aura, occurring three times a month for the past nine months, consistent with migraine without aura. On examination of the right upper arm, there is notable stiffness and palpable calcification in the muscle, consistent with other ossification of muscle. Additionally, a biopsy-proven Merkel cell carcinoma was identified on the left lower eyelid.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with gross hematuria and flank pain, concerning for a malignant renal pelvis neoplasm. Additionally, the patient reports progressive swelling and pain in the left lower extremity, diagnosed as chronic embolism and thrombosis of the left deep veins. X-ray confirms an incomplete atypical femoral fracture of the left leg with delayed healing, seen in a subsequent follow-up.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.

Clinical note:
The patient presents with a history of metastatic lung cancer, now reporting significant abdominal pain and intermittent nausea. Physical examination revealed palpable abdominal masses. Additionally, the patient reports increasing pain and limited range of motion in the left shoulder. Fundoscopic examination shows a suspicious lesion on the left retina.

Assign all applicable ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes.
